channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Brewers outclassed by Ohtani and big-money Dodgers as rousing season ends with NLCS sweep
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Turned out the National League Championship Series was indeed a mismatch, just as Milwaukee Brewers manager Pat Murphy suggested. But not only in payroll and star power. On the baseball field, too. Where it really matters.
item.dc:creator: DOUG PADILLA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ohtani puts on 2-way Sho for the ages with 3 homers and 10 Ks to lead Dodgers back to World Series
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — When Shohei Ohtani's third home run rocketed off his bat and streaked toward the left-field bleachers, the few fans still sitting at Dodger Stadium rose frantically, as if every single seat in the sold-out building had received a shock.
item.dc:creator: GREG BEACHAM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Shohei Ohtani hits 3 homers and strikes out 10 in Dodgers' clinching 5-1 NLCS win over Brewers
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Shohei Ohtani propelled the Los Angeles Dodgers back to the World Series with a two-way performance for the ages.
item.dc:creator: GREG BEACHAM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Belichick's North Carolina tenure hit by heartbreaking loss to Cal after late fumble
item.description: BERKELEY, Calif. (AP) — The Bill Belichick era at North Carolina already had endured several lopsided losses. Now the Tar Heels have a heartbreaking one to go with it.
item.dc:creator: JOSH DUBOW, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Aces celebrate third WNBA championship in 4 years with parade on Las Vegas Boulevard
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — Eight years to the day the WNBA and NBA Board of Governors confirmed the relocation of the San Antonio Stars to Las Vegas, the Aces celebrated their third championship in four years with a parade down the famed Strip on Friday night.
item.dc:creator: W.G. RAMIREZ, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Louisville intercepts 4 of Beck's passes, tops No. 2 Miami 24-21 to end Hurricanes' home streak
item.description: MIAMI GARDENS, Fla. (AP) — Louisville tried a little bit of everything. A fake field goal. Having three different players taking snaps from center. A reverse that wasn’t a reverse. It was enough for the Cardinals to stun No. 2 Miami.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Minnesota beats No. 25 Nebraska 24-6, sacking Dylan Raiola 9 times
item.description: Darius Taylor ran for 148 yards and a touchdown, Drake Lindsey completed 16 of 20 passes for 153 yards and a score and Minnesota sacked quarterback Dylan Raiola a school-record nine times in a 24-6 victory over No. 25 Nebraska on Friday night.
item.dc:creator: Phil Ervin, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Larkin's overtime goal lifts Red Wings past Lightning, 2-1
item.description: Dylan Larkin scored on a breakaway 3:36 into overtime to give the Detroit Red Wings a 2-1 victory over the Tampa Bay Lightning on Friday night.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Mariners a win from first World Series, beat Blue Jays behind Suárez's grand slam for 3-2 ALCS lead
item.description: Eugenio Suárez hit a go-ahead grand slam after Cal Raleigh’s tying drive in a five-run eighth inning, giving the Seattle Mariners a 6-2 win over the Toronto Blue Jays and a 3-2 AL Championship Series lead.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Destin, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: 49ers' Brock Purdy out for third straight game; Mac Jones to start against Falcons
item.description: SANTA CLARA, Calif. (AP) — Quarterback Brock Purdy has been officially ruled out of his third straight game for the San Francisco 49ers with a toe injury and Mac Jones will start against the Atlanta Falcons.
item.dc:creator: JOSH DUBOW, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Column: The Bears only need to find one more offensive lineman
item.description: The Chicago Bears' offensive line has shown significant growth, but right guard Jonah Jackson has struggled to hold up in pass protection and run blocking, needing to improve in order for the team to avoid a letdown.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: US businessman Josh Wander, whose firm invested in soccer clubs, indicted for alleged $500M fraud
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — An American businessman whose firm invested in several European soccer clubs that struggled under its ownership has been indicted in New York on charges of financial wrongdoing in an alleged $500 million fraud scheme.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Spain tops FIFA men's rankings, Germany set to be among top seeds in World Cup draw
item.description: ZURICH (AP) — Spain still topped the FIFA men’s world rankings published Friday that show Germany back on track to be a top-seeded team in the World Cup finals tournament draw on Dec. 5 involving U.S. President Donald Trump. Germany rose two places to No.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Jaguars eye return of center Robert Hainsey for Wembley game against Rams
item.description: WATFORD, England (AP) — There are many reasons why the Jacksonville Jaguars would be happy to get center Robert Hainsey back from a hamstring injury for Sunday's game against the Los Angeles Rams. Head coach Liam Coen listed a bunch of them Friday.
item.dc:creator: KEN MAGUIRE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mammoth beat Calgary in home opener, 3-1
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - JJ Peterka and Barrett Hayton scored second-period goals, Karel Vejmelka made 19 saves, and the Utah Mammoth beat the Calgary Flames 3-1 on Wednesday night. Utah became the sixth NHL franchise to win each of its first two home openers.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Colorado Mesa's Olivia Reed Thyne triumphs despite injuries
item.description: Olivia Reed Thyne overcame physical and mental battles to break multiple program records for Colorado Mesa University, inspiring her teammates and now focusing on recovery for her senior season.
item.dc:creator: Titus Cleveland, KREX 5

item.title: Chychrun scores in OT to lift Capitals to 3-2 victory over Lightning for third straight win
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Jakob Chychrun scored 1:19 into overtime and the Washington Capitals beat the Tampa Bay Lightning 3-2 on Tuesday night for their third straight win.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Mahomes leads the Chiefs to a 30-17 victory over Lions, and a fistfight erupts at the finish
item.description: KANSAS CITY, Mo. (AP) — Patrick Mahomes and the Kansas City Chiefs knew they were going to have a fight on their hands when the Detroit Lions, riding a four-game winning streak and a whole bunch of momentum, rolled into Arrowhead Stadium on Sunday night.
item.dc:creator: DAVE SKRETTA, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Buccaneers win over the 49ers to get to 5-1
item.description: The San Francisco 49ers and Tampa Bay Buccaneers are winning despite a slew of injuries to key players. It sets up well for both teams down the road.
item.dc:creator: Josh Dubow, WFLA, NBC

item.title: BYU improves to 6-0 with double overtime win at Arizona
item.description: TUCSON, Ariz. (ABC4 Sports) - BYU came from ten points down in the fourth quarter to remain unbeaten on the year with a thrilling 33-27 win at Arizona Saturday night.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Inability to get runners on base in Game 5 leads to Cubs' exit from postseason
item.description: *Video above: Cubs fans feeling anticipation of Game 5 of NLDS. MILWAUKEE (AP) — For the only time in their entire NL Division Series, the Cubs failed to score in the first inning during Saturday night's winner-take-all Game 5.
item.dc:creator: STEVE MEGARGEE, Associated Press, WGN 9
